10 Steps to Selling a Home With  

Selling your home is one of the most important financial decisions you’ll make. The right strategy, timing, and expert guidance from   ensure you maximize your return while enjoying a seamless selling experience.

Contact Me

Step 1: Decide when to sell

In the Canadian real estate market, timing influences your final selling price. Let’s look at the key factors to help you determine the best time to list.

Is it a seller’s market or a buyer’s market?

When buyer demand exceeds available homes, it’s a seller’s market, creating stronger competition and an advantage for sellers. When supply exceeds demand, it becomes a buyer’s market, offering more choice and negotiating power for buyers.

When is the best time to sell a home?

Spring and fall are the strongest peak selling periods. However, a properly presented home can sell successfully in any season when marketed strategically.

How quickly do you need to sell your home?

The right time to sell depends on both market conditions and your personal situation. If you need a quick sale, competitive pricing can generate immediate interest. If you have more flexibility, you may choose to wait for stronger offers and maximize your return.

Should you buy first or sell first?

Buying and selling often happen together, making timing critical. With the right strategy, both transactions can align for a smooth transition.   can help coordinate timelines, structure conditions, and manage the process efficiently.

Step 2: Choose the Right REALTOR® to Sell Your Home

In Canada, REALTORS® are licensed professionals who must follow a strict Code of Ethics. Choosing the right real estate professional can significantly impact your home-selling experience and the overall outcome of your sale.

See what clients are saying about working with  , or reach out to start your own journey.

Step 3: Prepare Your Finances

Selling your home involves understanding how your mortgage, costs, and future financing options will impact your final proceeds. Being financially prepared helps ensure a smooth transition from one home to the next.

Additional Tips to Prepare Your Home for Sale

  • Estimate your net proceeds before listing
  • Budget for closing and unexpected costs
  • Get pre-approved if you plan to purchase another property
  • Speak with a mortgage advisor or accountant early in the process

Not sure what your budget looks like? Use our Mortgage Calculators to find out.

Check Mortgage Flexibility

Mortgage & Payoff

Most sellers use their sale proceeds to pay off their mortgage. Open mortgages can be repaid without penalty, while closed mortgages may include early repayment fees.

Portability & Buyer Flexibility

Some mortgages are portable, allowing you to transfer your rate to a new home, especially valuable if it’s lower than current rates. In some cases, a buyer may also assume your mortgage, making your property more attractive.

Buying Before Selling

If you find your next home before selling your current one, you may need bridge financing, a short-term loan that helps cover your down payment until your sale closes.

Taxes and Closing Costs

Depending on your situation, there may be tax implications when selling. Primary residences are generally exempt from capital gains tax, while investment or secondary properties may not be. Plan for closing costs such as legal fees and applicable taxes like HST.

Step 4: Determine Your Home’s Asking Price

Correct pricing is essential for a successful home sale. Strategic pricing attracts buyers, maximizes value, and supports a smooth, profitable transaction.

Your REALTOR® will determine a recommended price using a Comparative Market Analysis, which evaluates:

  • Recent sales of similar homes in your area
  • Current listings and competition
  • Market trends and buyer demand

Ready to see how much your home is worth in today’s market? Receive a precise, no-obligation professional market valuation tailored to your neighbourhood.

Get Free Home Valuation

Step 5: Prepare and Stage Your Home For Sale

Preparing your home properly helps attract more buyers and can increase your final sale price. A clean, well-staged home stands out in Canada’s competitive real estate market.

Additional Home Staging Tips for Canadian Sellers

  • Maximize brightness during shorter winter days.
  • Use sophisticated decor for broad market appeal.
  • Prioritize high-end photography to drive interest

Not sure where to start? Get personalized advice on preparing and staging your home for sale in Canada’s market.

Book Your Free Home Selling Consultation

Declutter & Organize your Home

  • Remove personal items and excess furniture to open up space
  • Clear closets and surfaces to highlight storage

Complete Essential Repairs and Maintenance

  • Fix minor issues like leaks, squeaky doors, and chipped paint
  • Address visible or major concerns, such as water damage or electrical problems

Deep Clean Before Listing Your Property

  • Clean kitchens and bathrooms, floors and windows
  • Keep your home consistently clean during showings and open houses

Be Transparent About Known Issues

  • Share any known issues
  • Build trust with buyers and avoid delays

Transparency builds trust with potential buyers and helps prevent delays or complications during the sale

Step 6: Sign a Listing Agreement

The Listing Agreement is a legal contract that allows your REALTOR® to market and sell your home. It outlines your working relationship, key terms of the sale, and how your property will be presented to buyers.

What the Listing Agreement Includes:

  • Representation & terms: outlines your agreement with the REALTOR® and duration
  • Listing & pricing: MLS® exposure, asking price, and commission structure
  • Property & showings: home details, inclusions/exclusions, and viewing instructions
  • Closing & legal: timeline, deposits, zoning, and required disclosures

Step 7: Let Your REALTOR® Market Your Home in Canada

Effective real estate marketing helps your home reach more buyers and sell faster.   uses proven strategies and online exposure to maximize your listing’s visibility in Canada’s market.

List Your Home on MLS® and Top Real Estate Platforms

  • List your home on MLS® for maximum exposure
  • Reach buyers across top real estate platforms
  • Use accurate pricing and optimized listing details with relevant keywords

Use Digital Marketing to Promote Your Home

  • Promote your home across social media platforms
  • Run targeted advertising campaigns to reach local buyers
  • Optimize your listing for search engines to improve visibility
  • Use email marketing campaigns to connect with interested buyer

A strategic marketing plan can significantly impact how quickly your home sells and the price you achieve.

Start Marketing Your Home

Step 8: Find a Real Estate Lawyer

A trusted real estate lawyer protects your legal and financial interests throughout the home-selling process. They ensure all documents are accurate and transactions are properly executed from start to finish.

Start by asking family, friends, or   for recommendations for experienced real estate lawyers.

While your lawyer handles the legal side, don’t miss opportunities on the market.

Set Listing Email Alert

Here’s how your lawyer helps:

Review the Agreement

Ensures all terms, conditions, and legal details are accurate before you sign

Run a Title Search

Confirms there are no liens, unpaid taxes, or legal claims against the property

Handle Closing Costs & Documents

Calculates and manages land transfer taxes, title insurance, and other legal fees

Register the Property Transfer

Transfer ownership and register the property in your name with the provincial land registry

Coordinate Mortgage Funds

Works with your lender to discharge your mortgage and transfer funds on closing day

Step 9: Review Offers & Negotiate

When an offer comes in, your REALTOR® will present it and guide you through the details, including price, conditions, and timing. You may receive a firm or conditional offer and choose to accept, reject, or submit a counter-offer.

How the Offer Process Works

  • Your REALTOR® presents and explains each offer
  • The buyer’s REALTOR® may handle negotiations
  • You can accept, reject, or counter the offer
  • Conditions may include financing, inspection, or the buyer’s sale

Negotiation Considerations

  • Adjust the price if the offer is close but not quite righ
  • Negotiate closing dates to fit your needs
  • Review all conditions carefully
  • Your REALTOR® helps protect your interests throughout the negotiation

Step 10: Close the Deal

Once your offer is accepted, you enter into a legally binding agreement, but the sale is finalized on closing day when ownership transfers and all legal and financial details are completed.

Your Closing Checklist

  • Notify your lawyer that the Agreement of Purchase and Sale is signed
  • Complete any remaining conditions (financing, inspections, etc.)
  • Arrange utilities, insurance, and moving services
  • Update your change of address (post office, banks, government services)
  • Keep your REALTOR® informed of any changes that may affect the transaction

On closing day, ownership is officially transferred to the buyer, and you receive the remaining proceeds from your sale. At this point, the transaction is complete.

Let's Talk!

Have questions or just curious about the process? I’d love to chat and help you figure out your next step.
